patch.value: GH-348: Windows nightly vitest worker fork exits unexpectedly during teardown (all 246/247 test files pass)

Nightly Validation windows-latest/Node20 fails with a single vitest 'Worker forks emitted error / Worker exited unexpectedly' unhandled error AFTER 246 of 247 test files pass (0 assertion failures). A worker process crashes during teardown, almost certainly a native resource (LanceDB or OTLP gRPC exporter) not releasing cleanly on Windows. Distinct symptom from pm-zpe7 (OTLP cleanup EBUSY) and pm-i84i (file:// TS import SyntaxError). Non-blocking for releases (auto-release green; npm 2026.6.23 published). Needs a Windows runner to reproduce/fix confidently; default vitest forks pool, no isolate/teardown override in vitest.config.ts.

value.author: codex
value.text: Continuation check (codex): GH-348 still open and remains separate from the SDK version-compatibility branch. Current main CI run 28028252104 had Windows regression (Node 20) green, while GH-348 describes intermittent Nightly Validation worker teardown after tests pass. No SDK version-compat code changes touch the suspected LanceDB/OTLP teardown surface; leave pm-2kkl open for a Windows-runner reproduction/hardening lane.

patch.value:
## Triage 2026-06-24 (still open — needs a Windows runner)

Reconfirmed scope: GH-348 is a teardown-only worker crash on windows-latest/Node20 — all 246/247 test files pass with 0 assertion failures, then a vitest fork worker exits unexpectedly. Not reproducible on Linux. The per-PR `windows-regression` CI job runs only a 4-spec subset, so it cannot reproduce a full-suite teardown crash either; only the nightly full run exercises it.

Candidate mitigations to try (ranked, each requires a Windows runner to confirm):
1. Force single-use workers for the native-resource specs: `test.poolOptions.forks.isolate` is already default; try `test.poolOptions.forks.maxForks`/`minForks` pinning or `test.fileParallelism: false` for the LanceDB/OTLP-touching specs only via a `test.projects` split, so a crashing worker is isolated.
2. Add an explicit global teardown that closes LanceDB connections and shuts down the OTLP gRPC exporter (`forceFlush()` + `shutdown()`) before the worker exits — the most likely root cause is a native handle (vectordb addon or `@opentelemetry` gRPC channel) keeping the event loop alive past teardown on Windows.
3. As a last resort, scope `dangerouslyIgnoreUnhandledErrors` narrowly — rejected for now because it would mask real teardown regressions.

Decision: not bundling a speculative vitest-config change into the TypeScript-first examples PR (pm-ax7z) — it is unverifiable on Linux and could destabilize the green Linux/macOS gates. Non-blocking for releases (auto-release green; npm 2026.6.23 published). Tracked here until a Windows runner is available to confirm option 2. Related: pm-zpe7 (OTLP EBUSY, fixed), pm-i84i (file:// TS import SyntaxError).

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Continuation note: GitHub issue #348 was refreshed by run 28083306766 on 2026-06-24 for windows-latest / Node 20. PR #353 / pm-2p7a resolves the live scheduled failure class by removing unsupported Node 20 nightly lanes as part of the Node >=22.18 TypeScript-only extension runtime floor; branch nightly matrix is Node 22/24/25 on Ubuntu plus Node 24 on macOS/Windows. Keep this issue open until PR #353 is merged to main and the next scheduled nightly no longer reports Node 20.

author: claude-code-agent

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text:
Root cause located (2026-06-25, via pm-xaib / GH-363). The teardown "Worker forks emitted error / Worker exited unexpectedly" is the detached telemetry flush spawn in src/core/telemetry/runtime.ts (scheduleTelemetryFlush) launching a child via spawn() WITHOUT a child.on("error", ...) listener. An asynchronous spawn failure (locked-down Windows runner, EACCES, missing runner) emits an unhandled "error" event that Node re-throws as an uncaught exception, crashing the vitest fork worker on teardown. The sibling search background-refresh spawn (src/core/search/background-refresh.ts dispatchRefreshChild) already had exactly this guard — telemetry was the lone outlier. Secondary aggravator: the unit test "covers non-inline scheduler gate and start failure fallback" deleted VITEST and launched a REAL, network-touching, untracked node flush child instead of mocking spawn, leaving an orphan process on Windows.

Fix shipped in pm-xaib: (1) add child.on("error") gate-release to the telemetry spawn mirroring background-refresh; (2) mock node:child_process in the telemetry test so no real orphan child is spawned; (3) add a focused regression test that emits an async "error" and asserts the spawn gate is released; (4) run tests/unit/core/telemetry/telemetry-runtime.spec.ts in the per-PR windows-regression Node 24 job so this class is caught before the nightly. Closing this issue is gated on the Windows nightly turning green after pm-xaib merges.

message: root-cause found for GH-348 teardown worker crash

author: claude-code-agent

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text:
Root-cause fix SHIPPED to main in #365 (squash 6f88efcc) via pm-xaib: scheduleTelemetryFlush now attaches a child.on("error") handler that releases the spawn gate, so an asynchronous detached-spawn failure can no longer surface as the unhandled error that crashed the vitest fork worker on teardown. The culprit unit spec (telemetry-runtime.spec.ts) was also switched from launching a real network-touching node flush child to a mocked spawn, and now runs in the per-PR windows-regression (Node 24) job — green on the real windows-latest/Node 24 runner.

Remaining gate to CLOSE: the teardown "Worker exited unexpectedly" only manifested in the FULL nightly suite, so final confirmation is the next scheduled Nightly Validation run (windows-latest/Node 24, ~07:50 UTC). If that run is green, close pm-2kkl; the windows-regression isolation pass + the root-cause fix make this highly likely. Keeping open until the nightly confirms (terminal-evidence policy, pm-e9zh).

message: root-cause fix shipped in #365; awaiting next nightly

message: Remove repo-wide coverage from linked pm test sandbox; coverage evidence is recorded as item comment

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Implementation evidence: hardened tests/unit/commands/test-runs-command.spec.ts so background-run signal coverage uses process.emit from the test process after worker progress is observed instead of child process.kill(process.ppid, SIGTERM/SIGINT), avoiding Windows Vitest fork self-termination. Added that spec to the required Windows regression slice in .github/workflows/ci.yml. Kept MCP dynamic package upgrade routing test dry-run only so coverage is network-free and stable. Verification passed: pm test pm-2kkl --run --progress (3/3 linked tests), node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage (256 files, 5132 tests, 100/100/100/100), pnpm typecheck, pnpm quality:static, git diff --check, pnpm contracts:check, pm health --check-only --summary --json, pnpm audit --audit-level low, pnpm changelog:pm:check with pm-changelog 2026.6.20, node dist/cli.js package doctor --project --detail deep --json, pnpm security:scan, Sentry/telemetry gate ok (0 critical/high Sentry, telemetry finish error rate 0.74%). Manual temp smoke passed in /tmp/tmp.ogpaLWgnKJ using isolated PM_PATH/PM_GLOBAL_PATH and dist/cli.js: init, create, claim, update, comment, linked test, health, validate, close, release.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Release gate evidence: pnpm release:gates passed after this change. It rebuilt the CLI, passed typecheck/static/docs gates, coverage 100/100/100/100, version policy, security scan, packed npx smoke (packages=11), and package-first dogfood (88 commands, linked test run passed). Semantic dogfood was skipped by design because PM_DOGFOOD_SEMANTIC was not set.
